title: Trapella antennifera
taxonomy: Trapella antennifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to South Africa and is also known as the "River Pearl" or "Water Pearls". This plant is typically found growing in moist or wet environments, such as along streams and rivers. The classification of Trapella antennifera places it within the genus Trapella, which is part of the broader family Scrophulariaceae. As a member of this family, Trapella antennifera is related to other flowering plants that have similar characteristics and growth habits.
